DECLARATION OF COUNSEL IN SUPPORT OF PLTFF'S OPPOS TO DEFT'S MTN TO STRIK PUNITIVE DAMAGES FILED BY PLAINTIFF ASHBY, CAMAREE A., SARA A MINOR, BY AND THROUGH MIKEL ASHBY HIS GUARDIAN AD LITEM THURMAN, KEVIN

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TURNER GRANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE MOTION IS UNTIMELY BECAUSE DEFENDANT ANSWERED THE COMPLAINT ON AUGUST 15, 2013. SEE CCP 435(B)(1). IN ADDITION, PLAINTIFF PLEADS SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES. "ONE WHO WILFULLY CONSUMES ALCOHOLIC BEVERAGES TO THE POINT OF INTOXICATION, KNOWING THAT HE THEREAFTER MUST OPERATE A MOTOR VEHICLE, THEREBY COMBINING SHARPLY IMPAIRED PHYSICAL AND MENTAL FACULTIES WITH A VEHICLE CAPABLE OF GREAT FORCE AND SPEED, REASONABLY MAY BE HELD TO EXHIBIT A CONSCIOUS DISREGARD OF THE SAFETY OF OTHERS." TAYLOR V. SUP. CRT. (1979) 24 CAL.3D 890, 897. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

Mtn To Strike Complt
Off Calendar Apr-23-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT TURNER GRANT'S Motion To Strike Complaint is denied. The motion is untimely because defendant answered the complaint on August 15, 2013. See CCP 435(b)(1). In addition, plaintiff pleads sufficient facts to support a claim for exemplary damages. "One who wilfully consumes alcoholic beverages to the point of intoxication, knowing that he thereafter must operate a motor vehicle, thereby combining sharply impaired physical and mental faculties with a vehicle capable of great force and speed, reasonably may be held to exhibit a conscious disregard of the safety of others." Taylor v. Sup. Crt. (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890, 897. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: A. James Robertson; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
